     The Numbers Business Report

Pete’s Dragon (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $76,233,151Details
International Box Office $61,535,824
Worldwide Box Office $137,768,975
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $12,270,038
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $15,540,701
Total $27,810,739
Further financial details...

Synopsis

For years, old wood carver Mr. Meacham has delighted local children with his tales of the fierce dragon that resides deep in the woods of the Pacific Northwest. To his daughter, Grace, who works as a forest ranger, these stories are little more than tall tales…until she meets Pete. Pete is a mysterious 10-year-old with no family and no home who claims to live in the woods with a giant, green dragon named Elliot. And from Pete’s descriptions, Elliot seems remarkably similar to the dragon from Mr. Meacham’s stories. With the help of Natalie, an 11-year-old girl whose father Jack owns the local lumber mill, Grace sets out to determine where Pete came from, where he belongs, and the truth about this dragon.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$21,514,095 (28.2% of total gross)
Legs:3.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$65,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,702 opening theaters/3,702 max. theaters, 5.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $105,404,449

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 12th, 2016 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: August 10th, 2016 (Wide), released as Il drago invisibile (Italy)
August 11th, 2016 (Wide), released as Pete's Dragon (United Kingdom)
August 11th, 2016 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
August 12th, 2016 (Wide) (Argentina)
August 12th, 2016 (Wide) (Poland)
Video Release: November 29th, 2016 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for action, peril and brief language.

Running Time: 103 minutes

Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, Whitaker Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

For years, old wood carver Mr. Meacham has delighted local children with his tales of the fierce dragon that resides deep in the woods of the Pacific Northwest. To his daughter, Grace, who works as a forest ranger, these stories are little more than tall tales…until she meets Pete. Pete is a mysterious 10-year-old with no family and no home who claims to live in the woods with a giant, green dragon named Elliot. And from Pete’s descriptions, Elliot seems remarkably similar to the dragon from Mr. Meacham’s stories. With the help of Natalie, an 11-year-old girl whose father Jack owns the local lumber mill, Grace sets out to determine where Pete came from, where he belongs, and the truth about this dragon.
